_doc: The provider/framework matrix as DATA — the api-doc->http-mcp mapping, not code. A host runner expands x_axes x frameworks into wire calls: physics picks the tool (http_request | bidi_command), endpoint+caps_template build the target, inject_auth says where the resolved profile credential goes BELOW the boundary. http-mcp stays 3 tools; this is specs/ data, the same discipline as the route priors. Verified live on 2026-06-24 via http_request/bidi_command + auth profile prod:adminltqa.

auth.resolve: auth profile (env LT_USERNAME/LT_ACCESS_KEY or gitignored auth/<profile>.json) — the secret never crosses the agent boundary

inject_auth_shapes.basic_header: WebDriver/Appium/HyperExecute over HTTP — Authorization: Basic base64(user:key)
inject_auth_shapes.caps_url: CDP/Puppeteer ws — user/accessKey injected into LT:Options inside ?capabilities=<urlencoded json>

endpoints._framework_build_alt: https://mobile-api.lambdatest.com/framework — host observed in QA-sanctum prod runs; public api-doc canonicalizes manual-api. Both reach the same App-Automation real-device framework service.

selenium-bidi.note: session created over HTTP (CALL); webSocketUrl:true returns a BiDi ws (CHANNEL) for bidi_command. se:cdp gives raw CDP on the same session.

appium.verified: wire-CALL-appium-vd-validate — apk_arena (com.example.apk_arena) on Galaxy S22 emulator, passed

puppeteer.note: raw CDP — bidi_command drives it; timeout_ms >= 60000 for cold-start.

playwright.note: CATCH: /playwright speaks Playwright's OWN protocol (a CDP superset), NOT raw CDP. bidi_command cannot drive it — a host-side Playwright client (8/pilot) must. For the raw-CDP path use the puppeteer entry.

cypress._doc: NOT HyperExecute, NOT a live wire. Cypress runs as a JOB: the lambdatest-cypress-cli zips the project + lambdatest-config.json and POSTs a build; results are pulled by GET. The public api-doc 'Cypress Automation > Cypress' section exposes only RESULT-fetch endpoints (captured 2026-06-24 over the channel) — the run is CLI-submitted, so this is a runner, not remote-control.

_smartui_build_status.note: visual-diff build status (NOT the espresso/xcui framework build status). Captured from public api-doc 2026-06-24.

espresso._doc: native Android instrumented suite. NOT an appium session — a framework BUILD: upload app+testSuite -> POST a build -> poll status. Verified from public api-doc (testmuai.com/support/api-doc/app-automation-api-real-devices/) driven over the channel 2026-06-24.
espresso.prereq_upload: POST framework_upload (multipart) appFile=<.apk>, type=espresso-android, name=<label> -> returns lt://APP... for both the app and the test suite
espresso.status_poll: App-Automation build-status endpoint (sibling 'Get Build Status') -> verdict; the miss is the gold.

xcuitest._doc: native iOS XCUITest suite — exact sibling of espresso (public api-doc: 'Execute XCUI test on the LambdaTest platform (Server 3)').
xcuitest.prereq_upload: POST framework_upload (multipart) appFile=<.ipa/.zip>, type=xcuit-ios, name=<label> -> lt://APP...

doc_capture.source: https://www.testmuai.com/support/api-doc/ (public prod, no auth to read)
doc_capture.method: driven over the CHANNEL (bidi_command/script.evaluate via http-mcp) 2026-06-24 — the api-doc is a client-routed SPA whose sidebar is a collapsed accordion: NOT in sitemap.xml (only 5 selenium pages are). Method that works: click button[aria-label^='Toggle ... section'] to expand a group -> the group renders its leaves as <a href> -> read the hrefs; then browsingContext.navigate directly to a leaf href (Cloudflare managed-challenge clears itself in the live Firefox) and read the <pre>/<code> curl blocks. Synthetic .click() on a leaf is flaky; direct navigate is reliable.

devices_list: fetch-available-devices-for-running-tests-server-2 — live source for the device/mobile_version x_axes

doc_capture.cypress_automation: result-fetch only (GET): zipped-report, command-logs, console-log, network-log, enhanced-mochawesome — see frameworks.cypress

verified_curls.uploadFramework: POST manual-api.lambdatest.com/app/uploadFramework  -H 'Authorization: Basic <enc>' -H 'Content-Type: multipart/form-data' -F appFile=<file> -F type=espresso-android -F name=<label>
